_status: DRAFT — DO NOT IMPORT UNTIL 48H POST-DEPLOY VERIFICATION
_consul_condition: Co3 — Promote after C0 v3 holds in production for 48h with no regression. Architect makes final call on promotion.

data.title: Sync Shadow Represents Observed Remote State, Never Intended Local State
data.rationale:
In a write-through-cache sync architecture, the in-memory shadow used to compute diffs against local must be populated only from observation of the remote (listener fires, successful-push acknowledgments) — never from the local write itself. Shadow = observation. Local = intent. If shadow mirrors intent, the diff collapses and writes silently fail to propagate.

SproutLab B3 (April 2026) is the canonical failure case: `_syncShadow[key] = _syncCloneDeep(val)` at `sync.js:471` inside `syncWrite` contaminated the flush diff baseline. For every single-doc edit, the flush computed `shadow === current` and produced no delta — the push never happened. Edits stayed local-only for ~3 days. On refresh, listeners treated stale Firestore state as authoritative and overwrote local with pre-sync-bug values, producing the 'data disappearing on every refresh' symptom.

Codex's WAL-vs-SHA separation is the affirmative pattern: the WAL entry records intent (pending/syncing/synced/failed states); the GitHub SHA field records confirmed remote state. They are structurally distinct, and no code path writes the SHA until a push round-trip succeeds.

The canon codifies the distinction: any sync layer in the Republic must keep observation and intent in separate fields. Conflating them is a structural bug even when it appears to work in single-device testing.

sessions.summary: SproutLab sync emergency C0 v3 — B3 discovered (shadow contaminated by local intent), four-governor review (Maren/Kael/Cipher/Consul), Option Y deploy chosen (delete household + reseed). Spec §6 reconcile unchanged; remains Session 1 work.
decisions: deploy procedure: Option Y (household reseed) over Fix 6 (reconcile-lite in C0)
open_todos: verify B3 fix in production over 48h
sessions.handoff: Next session: Session 1 reconcile (spec C1-C5) if C0 v3 holds. Not before 48h post-deploy.
